title: California: CCPA / CPRA deletion & opt-out rights
summary: California residents can require businesses to delete the personal information they hold and to stop selling or sharing it, and may appoint an authorized agent to make those requests. People-search brokers operating in California are generally covered.

specialMechanisms.title: DROP — delete from every registered data broker in one request
specialMechanisms.summary: California's DELETE Act created DROP, the Delete Request and Opt-out Platform: a single verified request that every data broker registered with the state must honor. Registered brokers are required to begin processing DROP deletion requests by August 1, 2026. It is the closest thing to one-button removal — and the state, not Errata, operates it, so we hold nothing.

disclaimer: General information, not legal advice, and not yet independently reviewed — verify the current process and your eligibility before relying on it. DROP is run by the California Privacy Protection Agency; check the CPPA site for the live request flow and current dates.
